label: Supported answer with exact citation
question: Which evaluation signals should a production RAG service track before shipping a new retriever?

contexts.title: RAG release checklist
contexts.text: A production RAG release should track answer correctness, citation support, context precision, refusal behavior, and latency. Citation support checks whether every material claim is grounded in retrieved passages.

contexts.title: Embedding cost note
contexts.text: Embedding cost depends on corpus size, embedding dimensionality, and refresh frequency. Batch jobs should record index build ids and model names.

label: No supporting policy
question: What is the company's refund window for annual enterprise contracts?

contexts.title: Invoice metadata
contexts.text: Invoices include the workspace id, billing contact, purchase order number, and payment status. The document does not define refund windows.

contexts.title: Support handoff
contexts.text: Enterprise billing questions should be routed to finance when a contract amendment or tax exemption is involved.

label: Conflicting retrieved context
question: Can the support agent automatically rotate a production API key?

contexts.title: Legacy runbook
contexts.text: The legacy support runbook says support agents may rotate production API keys automatically after verifying the account owner by email.

contexts.title: Current security policy
contexts.text: The current security policy says production API key rotation requires human security approval and must not be completed by an autonomous support agent.

label: Tool-use risk classification
question: What controls should be added before an agent can call write-capable tools?

contexts.title: Agent tool policy
contexts.text: Write-capable tools require tool allowlists, schema validation, dry-run mode where possible, idempotency keys, and approval boundaries for irreversible operations.

contexts.title: Incident review
contexts.text: The last agent incident involved a repeated write call without an idempotency key. The remediation added trace ids, approval checks, and per-tool rate limits.

label: Recency trap with older distractor
question: What deployment gate replaced manual release approval?

contexts.title: 2024 release note
contexts.text: In 2024 every AI workflow release required manual approval from the platform lead before rollout.

contexts.title: 2026 release note
contexts.text: In 2026 manual approval was replaced by an automated canary gate. The gate checks retrieval quality, p95 latency, and error budget burn, then triggers rollback on failure.

label: Numeric extraction under distractor pressure
question: What recall@5 threshold blocks a retriever release, and what was last week's value?

contexts.title: Retriever scorecard
contexts.text: The retriever release gate blocks deployment when recall@5 is below 0.82. Last week's offline score was 0.76, so the index refresh did not ship.

contexts.title: Latency scorecard
contexts.text: The latency release gate blocks deployment when p95 answer time exceeds 1.8 seconds. Last week's p95 was 1.4 seconds.

label: Benchmark claim not in context
question: Did the new reranker beat the public BEIR leaderboard?

contexts.title: Internal reranker result
contexts.text: The new reranker improved internal citation support from 81% to 88% on a private support-ticket evaluation set.

contexts.title: Evaluation caveat
contexts.text: The internal evaluation set is not comparable to public retrieval leaderboards because it uses private tickets and product-specific answer criteria.

label: Multi-hop answer across two passages
question: How should agent traces connect latency debugging with answer-quality review?

contexts.title: Trace structure
contexts.text: Each agent run stores spans for planning, retrieval, tool calls, model calls, and final response assembly. Every span records duration and status.

contexts.title: Quality attachment
contexts.text: The response span should attach evaluator scores for groundedness, citation support, and policy compliance so latency and quality can be reviewed together.
